countNucleotides.name: Count Nucleotides
countNucleotides.description: Write a function that counts the occurrences of each nucleotide (A, C, G, T) in a DNA strand and returns a dictionary with the counts. Return false if the strand contains invalid characters.

nucleotideCountEmptyStrand.name: Empty strand
nucleotideCountEmptyStrand.description: An empty strand should result in all counts being zero.

nucleotideCountSingleNucleotide.name: Single nucleotide
nucleotideCountSingleNucleotide.description: A single-character strand should correctly count the nucleotide.

nucleotideCountRepeatedNucleotide.name: Repeated nucleotide
nucleotideCountRepeatedNucleotide.description: A strand with repeated nucleotides should correctly count occurrences.

nucleotideCountMixedStrand.name: Mixed nucleotide strand
nucleotideCountMixedStrand.description: A strand with multiple nucleotides should correctly count each nucleotide.

nucleotideCountInvalidNucleotide.name: Invalid nucleotide
nucleotideCountInvalidNucleotide.description: A strand with invalid nucleotides should return false.

setupCounts.question: How do I set up the counts?
setupCounts.answer: Start with a dictionary with all four nucleotides (`A`, `C`, `G`, `T`) set to 0. As you go through the strand, you'll add 1 to whichever count matches.

validChars.question: How do I know which characters count as valid?
validChars.answer: The valid characters are exactly the keys of your dictionary. Using `keys()` to get them means you don't have to write the alphabet of nucleotides out twice.

checkEach.question: How do I check each character?
checkEach.answer: Loop through the strand one character at a time. For each, check whether it's a valid nucleotide before counting it.

invalidChars.question: What should I do for invalid characters?
invalidChars.answer: The spec asks you to return `false` straight away. There's no recovering once you see an invalid character. Just stop and return false.

keys.description: Returns a list of all keys in the dictionary (provided by level stdlib)
